theme: aiming a lighthouse array while keeping harbors dark
title: Lighthouse Lattice: Harbors Lit, Harbors Dark
strategies: checker-matching single comb: find the largest divisor d of N (d<=K) that divides every harbor bearing but no protected bearing, and fire only that one d-element comb (scores exactly the checker's own baseline, ~0.1)

textbook phase-conjugate beamforming: split the K-emitter budget into T contiguous sub-arrays, one per harbor, each phase-conjugated toward its own bearing to maximize gain there -- never reasons about the protected bearings, so it leaks whatever sidelobe the conjugate phasing happens to produce and gets zeroed on the planted trap cases

zeros-budget/gain-budget split (polynomial root placement): treat the array response as the array polynomial evaluated at N-th roots of unity; pick a protected-safe divisor d of N and spend the remaining L-1 = K/d - 1 combs (same d, brute-forced offsets and per-comb phases) purely on gain, since every comb of spacing N/d is EXACTLY zero at every non-multiple-of-d bearing regardless of its phase or offset -- hard nulls come for free, so the whole remaining budget goes to maximizing the worst-illuminated harbor
